game_name: Heroes of Might and Magic III: The Restoration of Erathia

danger_signals.visual_form: Hexagonal grid display of hostile unit stacks with faction-specific creature sprites
danger_signals.meaning: Direct military threat requiring tactical combat response

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark faction unit types and town visuals on campaign map
danger_signals.meaning: Evil invasive forces threatening kingdom stability

danger_signals.visual_form: Reduced resource counters and blocked mine/resource post icons
danger_signals.meaning: Economic vulnerability and reduced town development capacity

danger_signals.visual_form: Resource posts and towns with enemy banners/control indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of economic power and strategic position

safety_signals.visual_form: Town structures with player's faction colors and banners
safety_signals.meaning: Secure base for army recruitment, resource generation, and artifact storage

safety_signals.visual_form: Resource post icons displaying player faction colors with resource generators
safety_signals.meaning: Passive economic income and strategic asset security

safety_signals.visual_form: Hero sprite with large creature unit numbers displayed
safety_signals.meaning: Military strength and ability to contest threats or capture objectives

safety_signals.visual_form: Holy Grail artifact icon in hero inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Path to permanent town bonuses and significant strategic advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Royal insignia associated with Queen Catherine and restored kingdom
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle towns, royal architecture, human settlements
Western border territories with distinctive terrain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Capital city Steadwick sacked; enemy faction town markers replacing royal ones; red/dark banners on previously secured settlements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC heroes and armies redirect toward defensive positions; increased enemy unit spawning in contested zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Royal faction weakened visually; throne room/castle visual deterioration; leadership transition shown through campaign narrative cutscenes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Queen Catherine assumes control; player takes perspective of restoration campaign; allied factions become available for player control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Western frontier zones display contested territory markers; multiple faction armies visible in western map regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Neutral or fractious behavior from border factions; potential for alliance or conflict depending on player actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player faction colors dominate town and resource node displays; hero garrisons visible in fortified positions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Passive resource generation; town development menus become available; hero recruitment from town creature dwellings

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of eight factions displays distinct castle/town visuals: Castle faction shows medieval fortifications; Tower faction displays magical spires; Dungeon faction shows cavernous dark structures
Continent of Antagarich displays geographical divisions between faction territories; sacked cities show visual destruction or enemy banner replacement
Each faction recruits mythic/legendary creatures unique to that alignment; creature sprite design reinforces faction philosophy (e.g., angels for good factions, demons for evil)
Mines, forests, and resource posts display unique graphics indicating resource type; control banners show ownership without text labels
Underground caverns accessible through hero movement reveal hidden threats, resources, and alternative strategic paths

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Located in town garrison, visible on map with army stack numbers displayed
Smaller creature stack counts; reduced movement range visual feedback
Static positioning on neutral territory; no immediate aggression indicated
Movement toward player settlements or heroes; combat engagement triggers on contact
Resource generation icons active; recruitment dwellings available; building upgrade options accessible
Enemy faction banner displayed; no player recruitment options; conquest objective marker

notes: HoMM3 uses a classic 1999 isometric strategy perspective with distinct visual languages for each of 8-9 factions. World-state communication relies heavily on color coding (faction banners), creature type recognition, town architecture, and campaign narrative framing through alternating player perspectives. The game communicates danger through enemy unit presence and territorial loss; safety through owned settlements and strong hero armies. Political situation (invasion, civil conflict, border skirmishes) is conveyed through campaign missions, NPC dialogue, and map-level territorial control visualization. The subterranean layer adds hidden-world complexity to world-state representation.
